$schema-note: Source of truth for @sethmakes brand aliases. Each entry maps an `mk--<name>` glyph to a source icon (`<set>--<name>`). The build (scripts/build.mjs) resolves these to icon bodies and emits dist/mk.json (a real Iconify set, prefix `mk`). `why` is the rationale for picking THIS glyph for THIS meaning — it ships, per CLAUDE.md: the package is the documentation. Sources are Lucide-first; Phosphor (`ph`) only where Lucide genuinely lacks the glyph. Renaming a meaning to a different glyph happens HERE, once, and reskins every consuming app centrally.

watch.why: an eye = 'have you seen/watched this' — the core verb of a TV tracker; reads as viewing, not playback

play.why: the universal solid triangle for 'start playback' — no ambiguity across cultures or apps

pause.why: twin bars, the inverse of play; pairs visually with the play glyph at the same weight

check.why: bare checkmark = done/confirmed/seen; no circle so it composes inside buttons and list rows

close.why: a plain X is the unambiguous dismiss/close affordance for dialogs, chips, and toasts

add.why: plus = create/add one; the canonical 'new' action, terse to match the mono voice

remove.why: minus = remove-without-destroying (e.g. drop from a list); deliberately NOT trash

trash.why: trash-2 has the lid lines, reading as permanent delete — distinct from the gentler 'remove' minus

edit.why: a pencil means inline edit/rename; clearer than a generic gear for content-level changes

search.why: the magnifier is the one icon every user reads as 'find', regardless of layout

settings.why: the gear = app/section configuration; reserved for config, never for per-item edit (that's pencil)

menu.why: the hamburger triggers nav/overflow menus; expected affordance on mobile-first chrome

chevron-up.why: thin chevron (not arrow) for collapse/expand and disclosure — directional, low-weight, non-navigational

chevron-down.why: pairs with chevron-up for <details>/accordion and select affordances

chevron-left.why: low-weight previous/back-within-context (carousels, steppers) — distinct from arrow-left navigation

chevron-right.why: the 'drill in / more' affordance on list rows; reads as progression, not page nav

arrow-left.why: a full arrow = actual navigation (go back a page); heavier than a chevron on purpose

arrow-right.why: forward navigation / 'continue'; the weighty counterpart to chevron-right's in-context hint

external.why: the box-with-arrow signals 'opens a new tab / leaves the app' — accessibility and trust cue on links

calendar.why: dates: air dates, release schedules, watch history — the literal grid reads instantly

clock.why: runtime / 'time left' / recency; pairs with calendar for the two axes of time in a tracker

star.why: rating and favourite; the outline form so filled/empty can express score state

heart.why: 'love it' / wishlist — an emotional favourite distinct from star's rating semantics

info.why: the i-in-circle for neutral details/help; matches the status family's circular footprint

warning.why: triangle = caution by convention; shape alone signals severity even before colour (a11y under zero-border design)

error.why: x-in-circle = something failed/blocked; the circle ties it to the info/success status family

success.why: check-in-circle = operation succeeded; circular to sit beside info/error, distinct from the bare 'check' verb

user.why: account / profile / 'who'; the single-person glyph for the common case (not a group)

home.why: the house is the universal 'back to start/dashboard' anchor in app chrome

refresh.why: circular arrows = reload/sync; clockwise to read as 'again', not 'undo'

filter.why: the funnel is the established 'narrow this list' control for tracker grids

loading.why: a partial ring is the spin target; the .mk-icon spinner rotates this under a reduced-motion guard

cart.why: the cart = a groceries/shopping list — the one domain glyph a planner-and-list app leans on; trolley reads as 'to buy' across every locale, distinct from 'add' (plus) which is the act of putting an item IN it

lightbulb.why: the bulb = an idea/suggestion/'what to make' prompt — the iconic tab for a meal-ideas surface; a domain meaning Lucide draws plainly, reserved for inspiration, never for tips/info (that's the i-in-circle)
